titel: The Swedish National Audit Agency's report on "Industriklivet."
html:
In this document, the government presents its assessment of the observations and recommendations made by the Swedish National Audit Office (Riksrevisionen) in the report "Industriklivet - Planning, Implementation, and Follow-up" (RiR 2024:17). The Swedish National Audit Office's overall conclusion is that both the government's design of, and the Swedish Energy Agency's work on, Industriklivet have several shortcomings. The Swedish National Audit Office therefore assesses that there is a risk that Industriklivet will not effectively contribute to either the industrial sector's transition to a low-carbon economy or Sweden's climate goals.

The government welcomes the Swedish National Audit Office's review of Industriklivet and agrees with some of the recommendations made. On January 16, 2025, the government tasked the Swedish Environmental Protection Agency, in collaboration with the Swedish Energy Agency, to review any potential overlap between Klimatklivet and Industriklivet, and to propose adjustments to the respective support regulations if necessary.

With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Office's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Agency has identified shortcomings in how the state supports the industrial sector's transition to a climate-friendly economy through the "Industriklivet" project. The government will now review how the support mechanisms work to avoid duplication of effort.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that reviews how the state uses its money. They have produced a report on "Industriklivet," a program designed to help industry become more environmentally friendly. The report shows that there are problems with how the government and the Swedish Energy Agency have planned and managed this work.

Because of these shortcomings, there is a risk that the money will not help Sweden achieve its climate goals effectively. The government agrees that there are areas that need improvement and accepts parts of the criticism.

To address the problems, the government has tasked the Swedish Environmental Protection Agency and the Swedish Energy Agency with investigating two different support programs. They will examine whether the programs "Klimatklivet" and "Industriklivet" are doing the same things and whether they need to be modified to avoid conflicts with each other.

The government has now completed its review of the report. This means that they have read the criticism and decided on the next steps to take to correct the errors.
